def: A trespass; a wrong done; the improper doing of an act which aperson might lawfully do. Bouvier. Wharton.
To bend the neck; to bow or duck the head. [Written also jookand jouk.]The money merchant was so proud of his trust that he went juking andtossing of his head. L' Estrange.

A headdress of linen or woolen, that hangs down over theshoulders, worn by old women of the lower classes; -- called also toymutch. [Scot.] "Having, moreover, put on her clean toy, rokelay, andscarlet plaid." Sir W. Scott.
Artificial digestion of food for use in illness or impaireddigestion.
Emitting, or appearing to emit, blood or sap, etc.; also,expressing anguish or compassion.
The inner planking of a vessel. Camp ceiling. See under Camp.-- Ceiling boards, Thin narrow boards used to ceil with.
A bay window. See Bay window.The beams that thro' the oriel shine Make prisms in every carvenglass. Tennyson.
One who, or that which, accelerates. Also as an adj.; as,accelerator nerves.
